usage: Pick ONE style. coarse: a single modulor_run tool (recommended; smallest prompt overhead). fine: one tool per op. Execute calls by running `modulor run <doc> -` with the commands JSON on stdin, or via the Python API (modulor.Cad).

function.name: modulor_run
function.description: Run Modulor CAD commands against a document file (2D drafting + 3D modeling; exports DXF/SVG/GLB/STL/IFC; renders PNG). Commands are executed atomically in order; each is {"op": <name>, ...params}. Use {"op":"help","name":<op>} to inspect any op. Available ops — add_arc: Add a circular arc, CCW from start_angle to end_angle (degrees from +X); add_box: Add an axis-aligned box solid; add_circle: Add a circle; add_cylinder: Add a cylinder (or cone, with radius_top) standing on its base; add_dim: Add an aligned linear dimension between two points; add_dim_angular: Angular dimension: measures the CCW angle at `center` from the ray toward p1 to the ray toward p2, drawn as an arc at `radius`; add_dim_radial: Radius dimension on a circle or arc: a leader at `angle` with the text 'R<value>'; add_ellipse: Add an ellipse; add_facade: Curtain-wall facade between two plan points: a mullion grid solid plus a glass pane solid; add_grid: Structural grid: labeled axis lines drawn in plan; add_hatch: Hatch the area of closed shapes with a line pattern (or a solid fill); add_implicit: Sculpt a solid from a math expression over x, y, z: the solid is where the expression is POSITIVE; add_layer: Create or update a layer; add_leader: Leader annotation: an arrow at the first point, a line through the given points, and text at the last point; add_level: Define a named building level (storey); add_line: Add a straight line segment; add_material: Create or update a material (used by 3D solids; exported to glTF/OBJ); add_opening: Cut a door or window into a wall; add_polyline: Add a polyline (open or closed); add_rect: Add an axis-aligned rectangle (a closed polyline), optionally rotated; add_roof: Generate a roof solid over a closed footprint: 'flat' slab, 'shed' single slope, or 'gable' double slope with a central ridge; add_room: Declare a room/zone/site: a named program area; add_sphere: Add a sphere; add_spline: Add a smooth curve through the given points (centripetal Catmull-Rom); add_stair: Straight stair flight as a solid; add_surface: Doubly-curved surface slab: z = f(x, y) over a rectangle, given thickness; add_text: Add a text label; add_torus: Add a torus lying in the XY plane (donut axis = +Z through `at`); add_wall: Add a wall along a centerline path; array: Repeat entities in a grid or around a center (polar); boolean_2d: 2D boolean between closed shapes; boolean_3d: 3D boolean between solids (walls are converted automatically); chamfer: Cut the corners of polylines with straight bevels at the given setback distance (in place); copy: Copy entities, translating each copy by `by` (repeated `count` times); define_block: Define a reusable block from existing entities; define_param: Declare a design parameter with a default value — sets it only if it does not exist yet; deform: Non-rigid deformation of solids (in place): twist about the vertical axis, taper along the height, or bend along x into an arc; delete: Delete entities; diff: Compare the current model against a saved snapshot: parameter changes, entity additions/removals/modifications, and metric deltas; doc_info: Summary of the document: units, layers, entity counts, bounding box; doc_new: Reset the document to an empty state; export: Export to a file; format from the extension; extrude: Extrude closed 2D shapes vertically into solids (one solid per selected entity); fillet: Round the corners of polylines with arcs of the given radius (in place); find: Spatial query: which entities are at/near a point, or inside a box? Results are sorted by distance; get: Full data of one entity, including geometry; help: Introspect the API: list all ops, or full parameter docs for one op; import_dxf: Import entities from a DXF file (ASCII, R12 through current); insert_block: Place an instance of a block: the block's base point lands on `at`, rotated and uniformly scaled; list: List entities (id, type, layer, tag and a one-line geometric summary); list_layers: List layers and their properties; loft: Skin a solid through a stack of closed profiles at increasing heights (straight/ruled between sections — add more sections for curvature, or run 'smooth' after); measure: Measure geometry: distance between two points, or length / area / volume / bbox of selected entities; mirror: Mirror entities across the line p1->p2 (solids: across the vertical plane through that line); move: Translate entities by a vector; offset: Offset closed shapes outward (+) or inward (-) by a distance; params: List design parameters, levels and the stored recipe size; program: The area program: every room/zone/site with its level and area, plus totals by kind and by name; project: Project solids/walls to a 2D outline region; recipe_set: Store the command list that generates this model (the design intent); regenerate: Rebuild the model from the stored recipe: geometry is cleared, parameters survive (optionally overridden), every expression is re-evaluated; render: Render a PNG image of the model so it can be inspected visually; restore: Replace the document with a previously saved snapshot; revolve: Revolve closed 2D profiles around the vertical axis through `axis_point` to make solids of revolution; rotate: Rotate entities CCW (degrees); scale: Scale entities about a center point; set_param: Set a design parameter; set_units: Change document units (does NOT rescale existing geometry); shell: Hollow solids into shells of the given wall thickness (in place), by eroding a copy and subtracting it; slice: Horizontal section: cut solids/walls at height z and produce 2D region(s) of the cut; smooth: Smooth solids in place: facet edges flatter than `angle` degrees are rounded into curvature-continuous patches (sharper edges are kept crisp), then the mesh is refined; snapshot: Save a named snapshot of the whole document (cheap insurance before risky boolean/transform sequences); snapshots: List saved snapshots of this document; solidify: Convert walls into plain solid entities (so you can boolean them with other solids); sweep: Sweep a closed profile along a 3D path to make a solid (tubes, ribbons, curved beams); update: Change non-geometric properties of entities: layer, tag, material; validate: Check the document for problems: degenerate entities, broken meshes, openings outside their walls, references to missing materials

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_arc
function.description: Add a circular arc, CCW from start_angle to end_angle (degrees from +X). [effects: doc]

center.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_box
function.description: Add an axis-aligned box solid. [effects: doc]

at.description: anchor point (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

size.description: [sx, sy, sz] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

anchor.description: 'at' is the min corner or the 3D center

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_circle
function.description: Add a circle. [effects: doc]

center.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_cylinder
function.description: Add a cylinder (or cone, with radius_top) standing on its base. [effects: doc]

at.description: center of the base circle (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

radius.description: base radius (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

radius_top.description: top radius; 0 makes a cone (default: radius) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_dim
function.description: Add an aligned linear dimension between two points. The measured distance is rendered automatically; offset places the dimension line to the left (+) or right (-) of the p1->p2 direction. [effects: doc]

p1.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

p2.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

offset.description: distance from measured points (default: 500mm equivalent) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_dim_angular
function.description: Angular dimension: measures the CCW angle at `center` from the ray toward p1 to the ray toward p2, drawn as an arc at `radius`. [effects: doc]

center.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

p1.description: point on the first ray (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

p2.description: point on the second ray (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

radius.description: arc placement radius (default: mean of the two point distances) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_dim_radial
function.description: Radius dimension on a circle or arc: a leader at `angle` with the text 'R<value>'. [effects: doc]

direction.description: leader direction, degrees (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_ellipse
function.description: Add an ellipse. Closed shape: it can be extruded, hatched and used in 2D booleans like a circle. [effects: doc]

center.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

rx.description: semi-axis along x before rotation (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

ry.description: semi-axis along y before rotation (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

rotation.description: degrees CCW (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_facade
function.description: Curtain-wall facade between two plan points: a mullion grid solid plus a glass pane solid. Panel sizes come from cols/rows or from target spacing. [effects: doc]

start.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

end.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

height.description: facade height (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

z.description: sill elevation (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

cols.description: panel columns (default: by spacing) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

rows.description: panel rows (default: by spacing) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

spacing.description: target panel size (default 1500mm eq.) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

mullion.description: mullion face width (default 60mm eq.) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

depth.description: mullion depth (default 120mm eq.) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_grid
function.description: Structural grid: labeled axis lines drawn in plan. Reference intersections anywhere a number is accepted: grid_x('B'), grid_y('3'). Axes can be explicit coordinates or {"start", "count", "spacing"} (spacing may be an expression). [effects: doc]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_hatch
function.description: Hatch the area of closed shapes with a line pattern (or a solid fill). The boundary entities are kept; the hatch is its own entity and re-clips automatically when rendered. [effects: doc]

boundary.description: closed shapes: circle / ellipse / closed polyline / region / room / wall footprint

pattern.description: parallel lines, two perpendicular passes, or a solid fill

spacing.description: distance between hatch lines (default: boundary size / 25) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

angle.description: hatch line direction, degrees (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_implicit
function.description: Sculpt a solid from a math expression over x, y, z: the solid is where the expression is POSITIVE. The native way to make organic and freeform geometry. Helpers: length(...), smin/smax(a,b,k) for smooth blends, clamp, mix, abs, min, max, trig. Example sphere: '500 - length(x, y, z)'. [effects: doc]

edge_length.description: mesh resolution (default: max extent/64; smaller = finer = slower) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_layer
function.description: Create or update a layer. Layers are also auto-created when first referenced by a drawing op. [effects: doc]

line_width.description: display/print line width (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

visible.description: hidden layers are skipped by render/export

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_leader
function.description: Leader annotation: an arrow at the first point, a line through the given points, and text at the last point. [effects: doc]

points.description: arrow tip first, text end last (2+ points) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

height.description: text height (default: 250mm equivalent) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_level
function.description: Define a named building level (storey). Reference it anywhere a number is accepted: "level('L2')" is its elevation, "level_top('L2')" is elevation + height. [effects: doc]

elevation.description: level elevation (z) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

height.description: storey height (enables level_top) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_line
function.description: Add a straight line segment. [effects: doc]

start.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

end.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_material
function.description: Create or update a material (used by 3D solids; exported to glTF/OBJ). [effects: doc]

metallic.description: PBR metallic, 0-1 (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

roughness.description: PBR roughness, 0-1 (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_opening
function.description: Cut a door or window into a wall. Position is the distance along the wall centerline to the opening center. [effects: doc]

wall.description: the wall: id, tag or selector (must match exactly one wall)

along.description: distance along the centerline to the opening center (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

width.description: opening width (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

sill.description: bottom height (default: door 0, window 900mm eq.) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

head.description: top height (default: door 2100, window 2400mm eq.) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

function.name: modulor_add_polyline
function.description: Add a polyline (open or closed). Closed polylines bound an area and can be extruded or used in 2D booleans. [effects: doc]

points.description: list of [x, y] points (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_rect
function.description: Add an axis-aligned rectangle (a closed polyline), optionally rotated. [effects: doc]

at.description: anchor point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

width.description: size along x (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

height.description: size along y (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

anchor.description: 'at' is the lower-left corner or the center

function.name: modulor_add_roof
function.description: Generate a roof solid over a closed footprint: 'flat' slab, 'shed' single slope, or 'gable' double slope with a central ridge. direction sets the ridge/slope orientation in degrees. [effects: doc]

pitch.description: slope, degrees (5-75) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

thickness.description: slab thickness (default 200mm eq.) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

overhang.description: eave extension beyond the footprint (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

z.description: eave elevation (expressions: "level_top('L2')") (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

direction.description: ridge axis (gable) or downhill axis (shed), degrees (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_room
function.description: Declare a room/zone/site: a named program area. It renders as a boundary + name + live area label in plan, and feeds the 'program' report. Boundary can be points or an existing closed shape. [effects: doc]

points.description: boundary polygon (or use 'boundary') (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

boundary.description: existing closed shape to take the boundary from

kind.description: rooms count toward the program area

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_sphere
function.description: Add a sphere. [effects: doc]

center.description: point [x, y, z] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_spline
function.description: Add a smooth curve through the given points (centripetal Catmull-Rom). Closed splines bound an area: they can be extruded, lofted, swept and used in 2D booleans like any closed shape. [effects: doc]

points.description: points the curve passes through (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

samples.description: curve segments between points (quality vs size) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_stair
function.description: Straight stair flight as a solid. Riser/tread are computed from the rise using the comfort rule (2R + T = 630mm eq.) and reported back; the flight runs along +X from 'at' before rotation. [effects: doc]

at.description: start of the first riser (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

rise.description: total height (e.g. "level('L2')-level('L1')") (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

width.description: flight width (default 1100mm eq.) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

direction.description: run direction, degrees (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

z.description: base elevation (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

riser_max.description: max riser height (default 180mm eq.) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

tread.description: tread depth (default from comfort rule) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_surface
function.description: Doubly-curved surface slab: z = f(x, y) over a rectangle, given thickness. The freeform roof/canopy tool — write the surface as math, e.g. a vault: '6000 - 0.0004*(x-10000)**2 + 0.1*y'. Helpers: sin/cos/length/smin/smax/clamp/mix. [effects: doc]

thickness.description: slab thickness (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

samples.description: resolution across the larger side (16-128) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_text
function.description: Add a text label. [effects: doc]

at.description: baseline-left anchor (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

height.description: cap height in doc units (default: 250mm equivalent) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

rotation.description: degrees CCW (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_add_torus
function.description: Add a torus lying in the XY plane (donut axis = +Z through `at`). [effects: doc]

at.description: center of the torus (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

radius.description: ring radius: center to tube center (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

tube_radius.description: tube radius (> 0, < radius) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

function.name: modulor_add_wall
function.description: Add a wall along a centerline path. Walls render as double lines in plan and extrude to 3D automatically. Openings (doors/windows) are cut with add_opening. [effects: doc]

thickness.description: wall thickness (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

height.description: 3D height (default: 3000mm equivalent) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

smooth.description: run a Catmull-Rom spline through the path points: curved walls

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_array
function.description: Repeat entities in a grid or around a center (polar). The original counts as the first item. [effects: doc]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

kind.description: rectangular lattice or around a center

nx.description: grid: columns (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

ny.description: grid: rows (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

dx.description: grid: column spacing (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

dy.description: grid: row spacing (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

angle.description: polar: total angle (degrees) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.description: 2D boolean between closed shapes. Inputs are consumed unless keep=true; the result is one region entity. [effects: doc]

function.name: modulor_copy

function.description: Define a reusable block from existing entities. By default the source entities are replaced by one instance in place (geometry is unchanged); pass replace=false to keep them and only store the definition. [effects: doc]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

amount.description: twist/bend: total degrees; taper: scale factor at the top (e.g. 0.4) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

plane.description: bend only: bend vertically (xz) or in plan (xy)

refine.description: mesh subdivision before warping (0 = none) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

function.description: Delete entities. [effects: doc]

function.name: modulor_diff
function.description: Compare the current model against a saved snapshot: parameter changes, entity additions/removals/modifications, and metric deltas. The review tool for design options. [effects: none]

function.description: Summary of the document: units, layers, entity counts, bounding box. [effects: none]

function.name: modulor_export
function.description: Export to a file; format from the extension. 2D formats: .svg .dxf (drawings, dims, walls in plan). 3D formats: .obj .stl .glb (solids + walls as meshes). .ifc exports a semantic IFC4 BIM model (walls with openings, storeys from levels, grids, rooms as spaces with areas, other solids as proxies) for Revit/Archicad/checkers. .png renders an image, .json saves a document copy. [effects: files]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

width.description: png only (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

height.description: png only (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_extrude
function.description: Extrude closed 2D shapes vertically into solids (one solid per selected entity). The profile entities are kept unless keep=false. [effects: doc]

height.description: extrusion height (+Z) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

z.description: base elevation (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

twist.description: degrees of twist over the height (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

scale_top.description: scale of the top vs the bottom (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

divisions.description: vertical subdivisions for twist (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_fillet
function.description: Round the corners of polylines with arcs of the given radius (in place). Radius is clamped per corner when segments are short. [effects: doc]

select.description: polyline entities (open or closed)

radius.description: corner arc radius (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_find
function.description: Spatial query: which entities are at/near a point, or inside a box? Results are sorted by distance. The fastest way to rebuild your mental map of a drawing. [effects: none]

at.description: query point [x, y] or [x, y, z] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

radius.description: with 'at': only entities within this distance (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

bbox.description: {"min": [x,y(,z)], "max": [x,y(,z)]}: only entities whose bounds overlap this box

select.description: restrict the search to these entities

limit.description: max results returned (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_get
function.description: Full data of one entity, including geometry. [effects: none]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_help
function.description: Introspect the API: list all ops, or full parameter docs for one op. [effects: none]

name.description: op name; omit to list all ops

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_import_dxf
function.description: Import entities from a DXF file (ASCII, R12 through current). Supported: LINE, CIRCLE, ARC, LWPOLYLINE/POLYLINE (with arc bulges), TEXT, MTEXT, SPLINE, ELLIPSE, LEADER, HATCH, DIMENSION, INSERT — plus layers with colors. Named blocks become document blocks with instance entities (semantic preservation); set blocks='explode' for flat copies. Unsupported entity types are counted in 'skipped', never silently dropped. This is how you take over an existing human drawing. [effects: doc]

scale.description: multiply all coordinates (unit conversion) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

blocks.description: map INSERT to block instances, or expand to flat entity copies

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_insert_block
function.description: Place an instance of a block: the block's base point lands on `at`, rotated and uniformly scaled. Use array/copy for repetition. [effects: doc]

at.description: insertion point (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

rotation.description: degrees CCW (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

scale.description: uniform factor (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_list
function.description: List entities (id, type, layer, tag and a one-line geometric summary). [effects: none]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_list_layers
function.description: List layers and their properties. [effects: none]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_loft
function.description: Skin a solid through a stack of closed profiles at increasing heights (straight/ruled between sections — add more sections for curvature, or run 'smooth' after). Profiles may differ in shape: each is resampled to the same point count. [effects: doc]

sections.description: [{"select": <selector>, "z": height}, ...] bottom to top, single-contour closed shapes

samples.description: points per section ring (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

divisions.description: smooth interpolation: extra rings between sections, traced with a vertical spline (8-16 makes flowing surfaces; 0 = ruled) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_measure
function.description: Measure geometry: distance between two points, or length / area / volume / bbox of selected entities. [effects: none]

p1.description: distance: first point (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

p2.description: distance: second point (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

select.description: length/area/volume/bbox: target entities

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_mirror
function.description: Mirror entities across the line p1->p2 (solids: across the vertical plane through that line). Set copy=true to keep the original. [effects: doc]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

p1.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

p2.description: point [x, y] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_move
function.description: Translate entities by a vector. 2D entities ignore dz. [effects: doc]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

by.description: [dx, dy] or [dx, dy, dz] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_offset
function.description: Offset closed shapes outward (+) or inward (-) by a distance. Produces a region; the source is kept. [effects: doc]

select.description: closed shapes (circle/closed polyline/region/wall footprint)

delta.description: + grows, - shrinks (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

join.description: corner treatment of the offset contour

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_params
function.description: List design parameters, levels and the stored recipe size. [effects: none]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_program
function.description: The area program: every room/zone/site with its level and area, plus totals by kind and by name. The brief-checking report. [effects: none]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_project
function.description: Project solids/walls to a 2D outline region. axis 'z' gives the plan footprint (x,y); 'x' the side elevation outline (y,z); 'y' the front elevation outline (x,z). Sources are kept. [effects: doc]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_recipe_set
function.description: Store the command list that generates this model (the design intent). After this, `regenerate` rebuilds the whole model from the recipe — typically after `set_param`. Use `define_param` inside the recipe for defaults. [effects: doc]

commands.description: ordered op commands; expressions in numeric fields are kept live

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_regenerate
function.description: Rebuild the model from the stored recipe: geometry is cleared, parameters survive (optionally overridden), every expression is re-evaluated. This is how a design stays coordinated when a parameter changes. [effects: doc]

params.description: {"name": value, ...} parameter overrides applied before rebuilding

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_render
function.description: Render a PNG image of the model so it can be inspected visually. mode 'plan' draws the 2D drawing; 'shaded' draws solids/walls in 3D; 'auto' picks shaded when solids exist. [effects: files]

mode.description: 2D drawing or 3D view; auto picks by content

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

width.description: image width, px (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

height.description: image height, px (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

labels.description: overlay entity ids/tags on the image so you can map what you see back to entities

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_restore
function.description: Replace the document with a previously saved snapshot. [effects: doc]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_revolve
function.description: Revolve closed 2D profiles around the vertical axis through `axis_point` to make solids of revolution. In the profile, x = distance from the axis (must be >= 0 after shifting), y = height (becomes z). [effects: doc]

select.description: closed profile(s)

angle.description: sweep angle, degrees (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

axis_point.description: 2D point the vertical axis passes through (profile is measured from its x) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_rotate
function.description: Rotate entities CCW (degrees). 2D rotation is about `center` in the plane; solids rotate about a vertical axis through `center` unless `axis` is given. [effects: doc]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

angle.description: degrees CCW (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

center.description: point [x, y, z] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

axis.description: rotation axis for solids (default [0,0,1]) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_scale
function.description: Scale entities about a center point. Pass one factor for uniform scaling or [fx, fy] / [fx, fy, fz]. [effects: doc]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

factor.description: number or [fx, fy] or [fx, fy, fz]

center.description: point [x, y, z] (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_set_param
function.description: Set a design parameter. Any numeric field in any op can reference it by name in an expression: {"width": "bay*2"}. Combine with `regenerate` to rebuild the model with the new value. [effects: doc]

value.description: new value (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_set_units
function.description: Change document units (does NOT rescale existing geometry). [effects: doc]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_shell
function.description: Hollow solids into shells of the given wall thickness (in place), by eroding a copy and subtracting it. Closed shells — combine with boolean_3d or slice to open them. [effects: doc]

select.description: solid entities

thickness.description: wall thickness (> 0) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

segments.description: erosion sphere quality (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_slice
function.description: Horizontal section: cut solids/walls at height z and produce 2D region(s) of the cut. Sources are kept. [effects: doc]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

z.description: section height (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_smooth
function.description: Smooth solids in place: facet edges flatter than `angle` degrees are rounded into curvature-continuous patches (sharper edges are kept crisp), then the mesh is refined. Great after loft/deform. [effects: doc]

select.description: solid entities

angle.description: edges with a dihedral angle below this get smoothed; above it they stay sharp (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

refine.description: subdivision level (2-6 typical) (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_snapshot
function.description: Save a named snapshot of the whole document (cheap insurance before risky boolean/transform sequences). [effects: files]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_snapshots
function.description: List saved snapshots of this document. [effects: none]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_solidify
function.description: Convert walls into plain solid entities (so you can boolean them with other solids). The wall entity is replaced. [effects: doc]

select.description: wall entities

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_sweep
function.description: Sweep a closed profile along a 3D path to make a solid (tubes, ribbons, curved beams). The profile is centered on its own centroid and carried along the path with minimal twisting (parallel transport); profile x/y map to the path's normal/binormal. [effects: doc]

smooth.description: spline the path through its points

twist.description: degrees of twist over the path (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

scale_end.description: profile scale at the far end (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

samples.description: points around the profile (number or expression string like 'bay*2')

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_update
function.description: Change non-geometric properties of entities: layer, tag, material. [effects: doc]

select.description: selector: "all", an id like "e3", a list of ids, or {"layers": [...], "types": [...], "tags": [...]}

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)

function.name: modulor_validate
function.description: Check the document for problems: degenerate entities, broken meshes, openings outside their walls, references to missing materials. [effects: none]

doc.description: path to the .json document (created if missing)
